description: 35M smoker with HTN. Too young for USPSTF statin primary prevention (requires age 40-75). No statin recommendation expected.

actions.label: Smoking cessation counseling and pharmacotherapy
actions.rationale: Current daily smoker at age 35. Smoking is the most modifiable CVD risk factor. Early cessation has outsized lifetime benefit.

actions.label: Optimize blood pressure management
actions.rationale: BP 144/90 mmHg with diagnosed HTN. No antihypertensive on medication list. Young patient benefits from early BP control.

actions.label: Lifestyle modification counseling (diet, exercise, weight)
actions.rationale: Young patient with multiple CVD risk factors. Lifestyle intervention is first-line before pharmacotherapy for primary prevention at this age.

contraindications.label: Statin initiation for primary prevention
contraindications.rationale: USPSTF 2022 statin primary prevention does not address adults under 40. Age-below-range exit should trigger.
